# eventType

**Type**

The type of transaction the line item reports, whether it’s a buy or refund.

## Declaration

```swift
string eventType
```

## Discussion

Allowed values: `BUY`, `REFUND`

The `eventType` field describes the type of transaction of the line item. Specify an `eventType` in the line item objects [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/OneTimeBuyLineItem], [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/SubscriptionBuyLineItem], and [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/RefundLineItem].

Use the `eventType` values as follows:

- `BUY`: The line item describes a product purchase, such as a one-time buy, or a subscription event, including when there’s no charge, such as with a free trial. Use this value in the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/OneTimeBuyLineItem] and [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/SubscriptionBuyLineItem] objects.
- `REFUND`: The line item describes a refund or chargeback, up to the full amount of the `BUY` transaction in the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/referenceLineItemId]. Use this value in a [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/RefundLineItem] object.

## Providing transaction info

- **creationDate**: The UNIX date, in milliseconds, that the customer authorized the transaction.
- **referenceLineItemId**: The line item identifier of another transaction, that the report  references.
